name: Salinas del Mesto Trail
name_original: Hinojares y el mirador al desierto del cerro de Atayuela -circular por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
description: Hike the moderate 7.3-mile Salinas del Mesto Trail in Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, featuring views from Cerro de Atayuela.
region_intro: Hiking around Hinojares offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra del Pozo mountains, part of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The region features an orographical labyrinth of valleys, limestone quarries, deep gorges, and high plateaus. Hydrological elements like the Turrillas River contribute to its natural character, alongside European Black Pine and Aleppo Pine forests. Best hiking trails around Hinojares The most popular hiking route is Cueva de la Virgen del Agua y el Pilón Azul - circular by the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete, featuring a cave and waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Enchanted forest las Higueras - circular from Pozo Alcón, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path through a unique forest environment. Local hikers also love the Salinas del Mesto Trail, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through the natural park with scenic views from Cerro de Atayuela, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Hinojares is defined by mountain scenery, limestone quarries, and significant rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more demanding hikes. The routes in Hinojares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Hinojares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Hinojares?
A: Hinojares offers a diverse network of nearly 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 10 easy, 7 moderate, and 1 challenging option, allowing you to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Hinojares?
A: Yes, Hinojares has several easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27628132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilón Azul Waterfall – Cueva del Agua loop from Río Tíscar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8 km walk that takes about 1 hour 16 minutes, featuring a picturesque waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua – Pilón Azul Waterfall loop from Castillo de Tiscar&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.1 km route that can be completed in just over an hour.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Hinojares area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails around Hinojares are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984902363/cueva-de-la-virgen-del-agua-y-el-pilon-azul-circular-by-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Virgen del Agua y el Pilón Azul&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.3 km long and takes around 2 hours 34 minutes, featuring a cave and waterfall. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983032460/enchanted-forest-las-higueras-circular-from-pozo-alcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted forest las Higueras&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.2 km moderate path through a unique forest environment.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or caves to explore on the hikes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its hydrological features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Virgin of Tíscar and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is accessible via walkways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984902363/cueva-de-la-virgen-del-agua-y-el-pilon-azul-circular-by-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Virgen del Agua y el Pilón Azul&lt;/a&gt; trail specifically highlights a cave and waterfall, and the Salto del Pilón Azul is a picturesque 10-meter waterfall within a stone canyon.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while hiking in Hinojares?
A: Hinojares offers a rich tapestry of natural features. You'll encounter an 'orographical labyrinth' of valleys, dramatic limestone quarries, deep gorges, and high plateaus. Notable landmarks include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pine of the Crosses&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Herradura&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also home to ancient salt flats, the Salinas de Hinojares.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Hinojares?
A: The hiking routes in Hinojares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from mountain scenery to unique forest environments, and the presence of natural attractions like caves and waterfalls. The well-maintained trails and the variety of options for different skill levels are also often highlighted.

Q: Are there any refuges or huts along the trails in Hinojares?
A: Yes, the area is part of the GR-247 trail network, which includes several well-maintained refuges. One such example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229805" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Zamora Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, offering a place for rest and shelter for hikers traversing the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas mountains.

Q: What is the terrain like for hiking in Hinojares?
A: The terrain around Hinojares is incredibly varied, ranging from mountain scenery to semi-arid landscapes. You'll navigate through an 'orographical labyrinth' of valleys, limestone quarries, deep gorges, and high plateaus. The presence of the Turrillas River and other streams adds lushness, contrasting with areas featuring European Black Pine and Aleppo Pine forests.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Hinojares?
A: Hinojares, being in southeastern Jaén province, experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons also showcase the region's botanical richness.

Q: Are there opportunities for bird watching while hiking in Hinojares?
A: Yes, Hinojares is an excellent location for bird watching. The rich biodiversity and varied landscapes attract a wide array of bird species. Hikers can often spot soaring eagles, vultures, kites, Golden Orioles, Rollers, and Hoopoes, making it a rewarding experience for ornithology enthusiasts.

Q: What is the longest moderate trail in Hinojares?
A: The longest moderate trail mentioned is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984885142/salinas-del-Mesto-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas del Mesto Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 11.7 km (7.3 miles) and typically takes about 3 hours and 17 minutes to complete. It leads through the natural park, offering scenic views from Cerro de Atayuela.

Q: Can I find historical sites near the hiking trails in Hinojares?
A: Yes, the area around Hinojares also offers historical interest. The Necropolis de Castellones de Ceal, an important Iberian settlement, provides a historical dimension to the natural surroundings, allowing hikers to combine outdoor activity with cultural exploration.
tips:
Village and municipality of the province of Jaén, belonging to the county of Sierra de Cazorla. It borders the municipalities of Pozo Alcón, Huesa and Quesada and by its term the rivers Guadiana Menor, Ceal, Turrillas flow.

Almost the entire municipality is part of the nature park of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas.
Small foray into the sandstone desert located on the Atayuela hill.

faqs.question: Where does the Salinas del Mesto Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail begins near the village of Hinojares. Parking is typically available in or near the village, which serves as a common access point for routes in this area of the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas.

faqs.question: Is the Salinas del Mesto Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While not overly strenuous, it involves a distance of approximately 11.7 km and an elevation gain of about 230 meters, requiring a reasonable level of fitness and sure-footedness. Beginners with some hiking experience should be able to manage it.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain typical of the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas. You can expect paths through natural landscapes, potentially with some rolling sections. The highlight View of the Desert at Cerro de Atayuela suggests scenic vistas, possibly overlooking more arid or open landscapes within the park.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the charming village of Hinojares, which serves as the starting and ending point, and the View of the Desert at Cerro de Atayuela, offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. Approximately 59% of the route follows the 'Sendero de las Salinas del Mesto', and about 37% uses the 'Sendero Hinojares - Cuenca'.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in southern Spain, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant times to hike, offering milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler with potential for rain.

faqs.question: Is the Salinas del Mesto Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking individual trails like Salinas del Mesto within the Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas. However, it's always advisable to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific access restrictions, especially during peak seasons or for certain activities.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when hiking in Parque Natural de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas?
faqs.answer: When hiking in the natural park, it's important to respect the environment. Key regulations include staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, refraining from littering, and being mindful of fire risks, especially during dry seasons. Camping is usually restricted to designated areas, and drones may require special permission. Always carry out what you carry in.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this length (around 3 hours 17 minutes), essential items include sufficient water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device (like komoot) is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Hinojares, a small village. You may find basic amenities such as local eateries or small shops there, but it's best to come prepared with all necessary supplies.

waypoints.name: View of the Desert at Cerro de Atayuela
